commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c792fcd8db30d92737a4ae03b12a1368832e381e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes `getActivePluginRegistry()` fallback resolution from `createChannelRegistryLoader` and changes the channel pinning test to expect outbound lookup to use only the pinned channel registry.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: use a pinned setup-only channel entry with no outbound, set the active registry to a runtime entry with outbound, then call outbound adapter resolution. I did not run tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor or maintainer follow-up before merge because real behavior proof is insufficient and the bootstrap compatibility contract needs an explicit patch decision.

target.title: fix(channels): remove unstable active registry fallback from outbound adapter resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84568

`registry-loader.ts` fell back from the pinned channel registry to `getActivePluginRegistry()` when resolving outbound adapters. The active registry is **unstable** — it gets replaced every time `loadOpenClawPlugins()` runs (config-schema reads, plugin status queries, tool-list loads, cron jobs, health-monitor checks, etc.). After such a replacement the new registry may no longer contain the channel's outbound adapter, causing:

```
Outbound not configured for channel: discord
```

Inbound and reactions still worked because they use a different resolution path.

This is a regression introduced by `d7896ed4c9` (`ci: retry release artifact downloads`), which re-introduced the fallback that was previously removed in `bc9c074b2c` (`fix(channels): use pinned channel registry for outbound adapter resolution`).

## Changes

- **`src/channels/plugins/registry-loader.ts`**: Remove the `getActivePluginRegistry` import and fallback branch. Only use `getActivePluginChannelRegistry()` (pinned registry).
- **`src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts`**: Update the test that asserted the unsafe fallback behavior → now asserts that the pinned registry is the **only** source for outbound resolution. Added regression comment referencing #84568.

## Local verification

Patched the installed `2026.5.19-beta.2` npm package (`load-yRKrJeVQ.js`) with this exact change. All five Discord bots (assistant, coder, default, researcher, writer) successfully receive **and reply** to messages after the patch. No `Outbound not configured` errors in logs.

## Tests

```
✓ src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts (14/14 passed)
✓ src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugins-core.loader.contract.test.ts (5/5 passed)
✓ src/plugins/loader.test.ts (passed)
✓ src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts (8/8 passed)
✓ pnpm build (success)
```
target.author: chouzz
